colorPairingRules.principle: Complementary color principle: mats in subtle complementary tones create visual harmony. Blue artwork pairs with warm cream/sand mats. Red/orange artwork pairs with cool gray or sage mats. Green artwork pairs with warm neutral or blush mats.

Value contrast rule: The mat should provide contrast with the artwork edges. If the artwork has dark edges, use a lighter mat. If the artwork has light edges, a slightly darker or medium-toned mat creates definition. The goal is clear visual separation between art and mat.

The 60-30-10 rule for framing: 60% should be the artwork (the star), 30% should be the mat (supporting role), 10% should be the frame (the container). If any element overpowers this ratio, the presentation is off-balance.

Monochromatic artworks (mostly one color family) benefit from mats and frames that introduce subtle contrast. An all-blue painting needs warmth in the framing — cream mat, warm wood frame. An all-warm painting needs a touch of coolness — white mat, silver or gray frame.

High-saturation artwork needs neutral framing to give the eye relief. Low-saturation artwork can tolerate slightly more color in the framing. The more colorful the art, the more neutral the presentation should be.

warmArtwork.description: Artwork dominated by reds, oranges, yellows, warm browns, gold tones
warmArtwork.matRecommendation: Warm-toned mats: cream, ivory, antique white, sand, warm gray
warmArtwork.mouldingRecommendation: Warm wood tones (walnut, cherry, mahogany), antiqued gold, warm bronze, espresso
warmArtwork.avoid: Cool white mats, silver frames, blue-toned grays

coolArtwork.description: Artwork dominated by blues, greens, purples, cool grays
coolArtwork.matRecommendation: Cool or neutral mats: white, cool gray, ice blue (subtle), soft silver-gray
coolArtwork.mouldingRecommendation: Silver, pewter, brushed aluminum, cool gray wood, black, white
coolArtwork.avoid: Warm gold frames, orange-toned wood, yellow-toned cream mats

mixedTemperature.description: Artwork with both warm and cool elements
mixedTemperature.matRecommendation: Neutral mats: off-white, light warm gray — neutral enough to bridge both temperatures
mixedTemperature.mouldingRecommendation: Natural wood in medium tones, charcoal, dark gray — avoids committing to warm or cool
mixedTemperature.avoid: Strongly warm or strongly cool choices that emphasize one temperature over the other
